questions.question: A ball at rest is dropped from the top of a 20 m high building. Calculate the speed with which it hits the ground. (Take g = 10 m/s²)
questions.option_1: 40 m/s
questions.option_2: 14 m/s
questions.option_3: 10 m/s
questions.option_4: 20 m/s

questions.question: A ball is placed at the edge of a table and left undisturbed. Which form of energy does the ball primarily possess due to its position, and what can happen if it is pushed off the table?
questions.option_1: The ball possesses chemical energy and will release it when it falls.
questions.option_2: The ball possesses kinetic energy and will remain at rest unless a force acts on it.
questions.option_3: The ball possesses potential energy and will convert it to kinetic energy as it falls.
questions.option_4: The ball possesses elastic potential energy due to its shape.

questions.question: A bi-convex lens forms a real, inverted image twice as large as the object. Where is the object located relative to the lens?
questions.option_1: At twice the focal length from the lens
questions.option_2: Beyond twice the focal length from the lens
questions.option_3: Between one focal length and twice the focal length from the lens
questions.option_4: At the focal length from the lens

questions.question: A car suddenly changes direction while moving at constant speed. Which aspect of inertia is being demonstrated, and how does the car’s mass influence this effect?
questions.option_1: The inertia of speed is demonstrated, and a lighter car resists direction change more.
questions.option_2: The inertia of rest is demonstrated, and the car’s mass does not affect direction changes.
questions.option_3: Mass only affects speed, not direction, when a car turns.
questions.option_4: The inertia of direction is demonstrated, and a car with greater mass will resist the change in direction more.

questions.question: A dentist uses a mirror to obtain a large image of a patient’s tooth. Which mirror should the dentist use and why?
questions.option_1: Concave mirror, because it gives a magnified image
questions.option_2: Spherical mirror, because it gives a virtual image
questions.option_3: Plane mirror, because it gives a real image
questions.option_4: Convex mirror, because it gives a magnified image

questions.question: A designer wants to create a device that spreads light from a small bulb to illuminate a wide area uniformly. Which lens should be used and why?
questions.option_1: A concave lens, because it diverges light rays outward
questions.option_2: A convex lens, because it converges light rays
questions.option_3: A plane glass, because it transmits light without bending
questions.option_4: A prism, because it bends light at an angle

questions.question: A device operates at 2 amperes current and 220 volts potential difference for 5 hours. How much electrical energy does it consume, expressed in kilowatt hours (kWh)?
questions.option_1: 1.1 kWh
questions.option_2: 0.22 kWh
questions.option_3: 5 kWh
questions.option_4: 2.2 kWh

questions.question: A passenger in a moving car feels a jerk backward when the car suddenly starts. How does the first law of motion explain this experience?
questions.option_1: The passenger tends to remain at rest while the car moves forward, showing inertia.
questions.option_2: The passenger is pushed backward by the air inside the car.
questions.option_3: The seat pushes the passenger forward, causing the sensation.
questions.option_4: Friction from the seat creates the backward jerk.

questions.question: A person with both myopia and hypermetropia is most likely to use which type of lens?
questions.option_1: Convex lens
questions.option_2: Concave lens
questions.option_3: Cylindrical lens
questions.option_4: Bi-focal lens

questions.question: A physicist uses a convex lens to focus sunlight onto a piece of paper and notices the smallest, brightest spot forms at a certain distance from the lens. Which property of the lens does this experiment help to determine?
questions.option_1: The experiment helps to determine the focal length of the lens.
questions.option_2: The experiment helps to determine the principal axis of the lens.
questions.option_3: The experiment helps to determine the optical center of the lens.
questions.option_4: The experiment helps to determine the diameter of the lens.

questions.question: A room heater and an electric bulb both convert electrical energy to heat, but why does only the bulb emit significant visible light?
questions.option_1: The heater is made of a different material
questions.option_2: The bulb's filament reaches a higher temperature, emitting visible light
questions.option_3: The heater uses magnetic effect instead of heating effect
questions.option_4: The circuit design is different

questions.question: A scientist wants to demonstrate that dispersion depends on wavelength. She shines monochromatic red light through a prism. What does she observe and why?
questions.option_1: Red light is absorbed, so nothing emerges
questions.option_2: Only a single red ray emerges; no spectrum forms because there is only one wavelength
questions.option_3: A full spectrum appears, showing all colors
questions.option_4: Multiple red rays emerge, each at a different angle

questions.question: A security mirror in a store is required to provide a wide field of view and always form diminished, upright images regardless of the position of objects. Which type of mirror and principle should be used for its design?
questions.option_1: Concave mirror using the mirror formula for real, inverted images
questions.option_2: Concave mirror for magnification
questions.option_3: Convex mirror using the mirror formula for virtual, diminished images
questions.option_4: Plane mirror for undistorted images

questions.question: A solar cooker uses a mirror to concentrate sunlight onto a vessel. Which type of mirror is used and what property makes it suitable?
questions.option_1: A convex mirror because it provides a wide field of view
questions.option_2: A plane mirror because it reflects light uniformly
questions.option_3: A convex mirror because it spreads light over a large area
questions.option_4: A concave mirror because it focuses parallel sunlight to a single point

questions.question: A sound wave travels from air into water. How does its frequency, speed, and wavelength change as it crosses the boundary?
questions.option_1: Its frequency decreases, speed decreases, and wavelength increases
questions.option_2: Its frequency remains constant, speed decreases, and wavelength decreases
questions.option_3: Its frequency increases, speed remains the same, and wavelength decreases
questions.option_4: Its frequency remains constant, speed increases, and wavelength increases

questions.question: A student claims that the force exerted by the Earth on the Moon is much stronger than the force exerted by the Moon on the Earth. Based on Newton's third law and the Universal Law of Gravitation, is this claim correct?
questions.option_1: No, Earth exerts a much smaller force on the Moon
questions.option_2: No, the Moon exerts a stronger force because it is closer to Earth
questions.option_3: No, both forces are equal in magnitude but opposite in direction
questions.option_4: Yes, because Earth is more massive, it exerts more force

questions.question: A student claims that two cars of equal mass, one moving at 40 km/h and another at 80 km/h, have the same kinetic energy if their engines provide the same power. What is the error in this reasoning?
questions.option_1: Power supplied determines energy, regardless of speed.
questions.option_2: The cars have equal energy if the engines are identical.
questions.option_3: Kinetic energy depends on the square of speed, not just on power supplied.
questions.option_4: Both cars have equal kinetic energy since both cover the same distance.

questions.question: A student drags a suitcase on a flat airport floor. If the force applied and the distance moved both double, how does the work done change?
questions.option_1: Work done halves.
questions.option_2: Work done remains unchanged.
questions.option_3: Work done becomes four times greater.
questions.option_4: Work done doubles.

questions.question: A student observes that a compass needle deflects when brought near a current-carrying wire. Using your understanding of magnetic fields, explain why this happens.
questions.option_1: A current-carrying wire creates a magnetic field that exerts a force on the compass needle.
questions.option_2: The wire produces an electric force that acts on the compass needle.
questions.option_3: The wire heats up, causing air currents to move the needle.
questions.option_4: The wire causes a gravitational effect moving the needle.

questions.question: A student uses a convex lens to project an image of a tree onto a wall. If the image is found to be smaller than the tree, what can be inferred about the tree's position relative to the lens?
questions.option_1: The tree is between the lens and its focal point.
questions.option_2: The tree is at the focal point of the lens.
questions.option_3: The tree is at twice the focal length from the lens.
questions.option_4: The tree is placed beyond twice the focal length (beyond 2F) of the lens.

questions.question: A technician needs to connect a new appliance in a domestic circuit to ensure it receives full voltage and can be operated independently. What should the technician do?
questions.option_1: Connect the appliance only to the neutral wire
questions.option_2: Connect the appliance in parallel with existing appliances
questions.option_3: Connect the appliance in series with existing appliances
questions.option_4: Connect the appliance only to the earth wire

questions.question: A young child is diagnosed with both myopia and hypermetropia in different eyes. What is the most appropriate corrective approach?
questions.option_1: Providing glasses with different lenses for each eye (concave for one, convex for the other)
questions.option_2: Using bifocal lenses in both eyes
questions.option_3: Using cylindrical lenses in both eyes
questions.option_4: Providing identical convex lenses for both eyes

questions.question: An electric refrigerator has a power rating of 500 W and is used for 6 hours each day. If the cost of electrical energy is ₹4.00 per kilowatt-hour, what will be the total cost of operating the refrigerator for 30 days?
An object is placed on a shelf 2 m above the ground. If the shelf is raised to 4 m, what happens to the potential energy of the object?
questions.option_1: The potential energy triples
questions.option_2: The potential energy halves
questions.option_3: The potential energy remains the same
questions.option_4: The potential energy doubles

questions.question: An object of mass 3 kg initially at rest is subjected to a constant force of 9 N for 2 seconds. What will be its final velocity?
questions.option_1: 6 m/s
questions.option_2: 9 m/s
questions.option_3: 18 m/s
questions.option_4: 3 m/s
